text.title: Terraform CI/CD Patterns
text.summary: Pipeline stages, saved plan rule, security scanning, OIDC authentication, and Atlantis configuration.

rule.statement: Run the full pipeline in order: terraform fmt -check, terraform validate, tflint, tfsec/checkov, terraform plan -out=tfplan, human review, terraform apply tfplan.
rule.rationale: Each stage catches a different class of error: fmt catches style, validate catches syntax, tflint catches provider-specific issues, tfsec catches security misconfigurations, and the saved plan ensures apply matches reviewed output.

rule.statement: Always save the plan to a file with -out=tfplan and apply that exact file; never re-run terraform plan between plan and apply in CI.
rule.rationale: Re-planning between review and apply allows infrastructure to change state, meaning the applied plan differs from the reviewed plan. This is a common cause of production incidents in automated pipelines.

rule.statement: Run tfsec and checkov on every pull request; fail the pipeline on any CRITICAL or HIGH severity finding without an approved exception; run Infracost to surface cost impact on PRs.
rule.rationale: Security scanners catch default-insecure configurations (unencrypted S3, public RDS, wildcard IAM) before they reach production. Cost estimation prevents unexpected bill increases from infrastructure changes.

rule.statement: Use OIDC-based authentication for GitHub Actions and GitLab CI to assume cloud provider roles; never store static access keys as CI secrets.
rule.rationale: OIDC tokens are short-lived (15 minutes), scoped to the specific workflow run, and automatically expire. Static access keys are frequently leaked in CI logs, artifacts, or public repositories.

example.code:
permissions: id-token: write contents: read
steps: - uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4 with: role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::ACCOUNT:role/GitHubActionsRole aws-region: us-east-1 - run: terraform init env: TF_IN_AUTOMATION: "true"

rule.statement: Configure Atlantis with per-project autoplan and apply_requirements: approved, mergeable; use Atlantis for teams that prefer plan-in-PR workflow over separate CI apply stages.
rule.rationale: Atlantis runs plan automatically on PR creation and posts output as a comment. apply_requirements: approved ensures at least one human review before any apply executes.

antipattern.description: Applying a re-generated plan (not the saved plan file) in CI after human review.
antipattern.reason: Infrastructure can change between the plan review and apply. Re-planning may produce a different plan than what was reviewed, allowing unreviewed changes to reach production.
